---
title: "Sites We Like"
description: "Projects we admire, learn from, and consult"
---

Canada Observatory didn't invent the idea of patient, non-partisan, source-cited public
data. These are projects we admire and regularly consult — some are role
models for what this site tries to be, some cover ground we don't, and a few
are sources we build on directly.

## Role models

- **[Our World in Data](https://ourworldindata.org/)** — the standard-setter
  for open, rigorous, beautifully explained data publishing, on global and
  long-run questions. Also one of our upstream sources (energy and
  consumption-based CO₂). If you like CanObs, you will love OWID.
- **[USAFacts](https://usafacts.org/)** — a non-partisan, government-data-only
  portrait of the United States, with a source link on every figure. The
  closest thing to Canada Observatory's mission, one country south.
- **[Gapminder](https://www.gapminder.org/)** — the late Hans Rosling's
  project against statistical misconceptions. A standing reminder that the
  data is usually more surprising — and less bleak — than the headlines.

## Canada

- **[CensusMapper](https://censusmapper.ca/)** — zoomable maps of any Canadian
  census variable, by a single dedicated developer. The deepest census mapping
  available anywhere; our neighbourhood maps cover a handful of curated
  questions, CensusMapper covers them all.
- **[Statistics Canada's Quality of Life Hub](https://www.statcan.gc.ca/en/topics-start/quality_of_life)** —
  the official well-being framework: 90+ indicators on prosperity, health,
  society, environment, and good governance, from the agency that collects
  the data.

## International data explorers

- **[OECD Data Explorer](https://data-explorer.oecd.org/)** and the
  **[OECD Better Life Index](https://www.oecdbetterlifeindex.org/)** — the
  upstream home of most of our peer comparisons, and an interactive way to
  weight what "doing well" means to you.
- **[World Bank Open Data](https://data.worldbank.org/)** — free, global,
  CC-licensed indicators; the source of our WHO- and UN-derived health
  comparisons.

*A listing here is our genuine recommendation, nothing more — there are no
partnerships or endorsements in either direction.*
